key_themes: Successful Versigent spin-off refocusing on higher-margin ADAS, electrification & software
Q4/FY2025 adjusted EPS & revenue beats with record results and $30B+ 2026 bookings pipeline
Persistent commodity/FX margin pressure, one-time impairment, and post-spin analyst PT reductions
summary: Aptiv delivered record full-year 2025 revenue of $20.4B (up 3%) and beat Q4 adjusted EPS ($1.86 vs $1.82 expected) with strong cash flow, while successfully completing the Versigent (EDS) spin-off in early April 2026 to create a pure-play technology company in automation, high-voltage EV systems, and software. New Aptiv 2026 guidance calls for ~4% revenue growth and adjusted EPS of $5.70–$6.10; analysts largely maintain Buy/Outperform ratings with an ~$88 average target (significant upside from the ~$60 share price), though many cut targets to reflect the smaller entity. Biggest risk is ongoing margin compression from commodities, FX volatility, and auto OEM demand softness.[[1]](https://ir.aptiv.com/news/news-details/2026/Aptiv-Reports-Fourth-Quarter-2025-Financial-Results/)[[2]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/NYSE/APTV/earnings/)[[3]](https://simplywall.st/stocks/us/automobiles/nyse-aptv/aptiv/news/a-look-at-aptiv-aptv-valuation-after-debt-tender-offer-and-v)[[4]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/APTV/)

recent_posts.highlight: TD Cowen maintains Buy on $APTV but lowers price target to $90 from $93 post-spin.
$APTV maintained at Buy by TD Cowen, signaling continued confidence in the company’s outlook.
Aptiv gaining attention as strong EV play despite recent declines; analysts optimistic on post-spin growth potential.
